stories.headline: Iran war day 113: Tehran presses US over Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran presses US over Lebanon as Israeli strikes test fragile ceasefire and broader peace efforts.

stories.headline: Polish president strips Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honour
stories.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki’s decision to strip Volodymyr Zelenskyy of the Order of the White Eagle has sparked controversy in Ukraine.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy issues one-week ultimatum to Lukashenka over drone-guidance equipment
stories.summary: During a media briefing in Kyiv on Friday, Zelenskyy threatened Ukraine will take down the drone relay equipment on the border if the Belarusian leader, Aliaksandr Lukashenka, doesn't do anything to address it.

stories.headline: US-Iran Delegations Expected in Switzerland
stories.summary: Three days after President Trump signed an agreement with Iran, Switzerland is playing host for diplomatic talks with the United States, Iran, Pakistan, and Qatar in attendance. The Associated Press's International Correspondent Philip Crowther joins Bloomberg This Weekend with hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini to discuss who may be attending this summit and what a recent strikes in Lebanon could mean for any deal. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Burnham May Yet Rewrite UK Fiscal Playbook If He Becomes PM
stories.summary: After Andy Burnham took a giant leap toward becoming the UK’s next prime minister, focus is turning to how the team he’s assembling could shape the country’s economic policies.

stories.headline: Ships Told They Can Use South Hormuz Route With Signals On
stories.summary: Ships can consider transiting the Strait of Hormuz along the southern route at any time of day with their signals on, a key naval information group said, as energy traders and shipowners look for clues about how quickly and safely Hormuz traffic can return to normal.

stories.headline: Odd Lots: How Substack Creators Are Thinking About AI (Podcast)
stories.summary: We closed out our New York live show on May 28 with a panel that featured three of our favorite Substackers: James van Geelen of Citrini Research, Sam Ro, founder of The TKer, and journalist Jasmine Sun. They’ve all been Odd Lots guests before, and we wanted to get them together to discuss how journalists and analysts are supposed to cover this incredibly strange and highly pressurized moment in markets. Not only has AI basically infected every c

stories.headline: Germany’s Weirdest Debt Market May No Longer Be Safe Haven for Lenders
stories.summary: After storied Austrian motorcycle manufacturer KTM AG presented an insolvency plan to creditors last year, several of them convened a video conference to discuss a counter-proposal. More than 100 parties joined, but none of the usual big corporate lenders were involved. Instead, the group featured a motley crew of backers from tiny German towns, Chinese banks and European pension funds, all with wildly varying knowledge of the case at hand. The c

stories.headline: Arizona Democrat’s Tough Past Fuels Pitch for a Key House Seat
stories.summary: JoAnna Mendoza, a Democrat challenging Representative Juan Ciscomani in a tossup district, is leaning into her painful and unique life story to appeal to voters.

stories.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Beto Coral’s criticism of a presidential candidate in his home country of Colombia undermined U.S. foreign policy.

stories.headline: James Bradley, Co-Author of ‘Flags of Our Fathers,’ Dies at 72
stories.summary: His best-selling book celebrated the servicemen in the stirring photograph of the U.S. flag-raising on Iwo Jima. One, it was long believed, was his father.

stories.headline: US, Iran Delay Nuclear Talks as Lebanon Clashes Worsen
stories.summary:
Iran delayed the start of negotiations over a permanent peace deal with the US after fighting intensified in southern Lebanon, a potential setback to Donald Trump’s efforts to end the war and curb Tehran’s nuclear program.
Ambassador Puneet Talwar, Principal at Talwar Global Strategies and former US Ambassador to Morocco, discusses the US, Iran interim deal and what he expects to see from negotiations in the coming days.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Italy's Meloni says Trump 'made up' story that she 'begged' him for photo at G7

stories.headline: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known

stories.headline: Trump visits new Air Force One, a luxury gift from Qatar that will soon fly him

stories.headline: Jordan Bardella meets Karol Nawrocki in Warsaw
stories.summary: French far‑right leader Jordan Bardella on Thursday began a two‑day visit to Poland, seeking meetings with right‑wing and nationalist figures ahead of next year’s French presidential election, after similar trips to Portugal, Italy and Belgium.

stories.headline: Sánchez backs international dialogue against other leaders: 'A Europe open to the world'
stories.summary: Spain’s prime minister used the European Council summit to urge a more ambitious multiannual budget, and voiced solidarity with Meloni after Trump said she begged him for a photo.

stories.headline: At the Web3 Summit, a growing movement wants to reclaim the internet for the people
stories.summary: At the Web3 Summit in Berlin, developers, economists and activists debate whether decentralised technology can deliver on its promise to put control of the internet back into the hands of users.

stories.headline: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
stories.summary: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

stories.headline: India's cash transfer boom gives relief to the poor but strains budgets
stories.summary: Cash transfers are a major welfare tool but they come at a huge cost, raising concerns about their long-term efficacy.

stories.headline: Value Investing Legend Seth Klarman: Masters in Business
stories.summary: Barry sits down with Munger disciple Seth Klarman, CEO of Baupost Group, a Boston-based investment manager with a multi-strategy approach. They discuss Seth's start as a 25 year-old and journey to CEO. They also discuss his approach to risk, IPOs, and sectors along with his sports passions including a smaller ownership in the Boston Red Sox, horse racing, and his feelings about the Boston Celtics' 2026 season. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Latin American FX Edge Higher With Markets in ‘Holding Pattern’
stories.summary: Most Latin American currencies advanced in a quiet session for global markets amid holidays in the US and parts of Asia, leaving assets in a holding pattern as traders await fresh news out of the Middle East.

stories.headline: Starbucks Cuts UK, Hong Kong Office Jobs in Restructuring Effort
stories.summary: Starbucks Corp. laid off corporate workers in the London and Hong Kong hubs that oversee parts of its international business, as the coffee chain gives third-party licensees greater latitude to run its stores outside of North America.

stories.headline: Canada Imposes Temporary 10% Tariff on Canned Vegetables
stories.summary: Canada applied a 10% import tax on imports of canned vegetables to protect domestic growers and food processors, the Department of Finance said in a statement Friday.

stories.headline: Global Stocks End Strong Week on Cautious Note
stories.summary:
Global stocks are ending a strong week on a cautious note as relief over an interim peace deal between the US and Iran gave way to a focus on the challenges of securing a lasting agreement.
Anneka Treon, Global Head of Private Banking, Wealth Management, and Investments at ING Investments, discusses her outlook for Europe, client concerns, and the broader macro picture. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: India Stock Regulator Lets Firms Buy Back Shares From Market
stories.summary: India’s securities regulator will reintroduce open-market buybacks for companies, a move that could bolster shares prices as local equities have lagged global peers.

stories.headline: Trump Shifts Stance in South Carolina After Losses in Governor’s Races
stories.summary: On Friday the president made the unusual move of endorsing both candidates in the Republican runoff for South Carolina governor, after initially supporting one of them.

stories.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

stories.headline: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
stories.summary: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.

stories.headline: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
stories.summary: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.
